gtmChallenges.challenge1: Functions are siloed, with no shared KPIs
gtmChallenges.challenge2: Revenue and pipeline forecasts are unreliable
gtmChallenges.challenge3: Differentiation is weak, competing mainly on price or features
gtmChallenges.challenge4: Customer ROI and outcomes are unclear, making renewals and expansion difficult
gtmChallenges.challenge5: Deal management and tracking lack predictability
gtmChallenges.challenge6: Over-reliance on new customer acquisition; existing account growth is stagnant
gtmChallenges.challenge7: Success stories and learnings are not shared, reducing organizational repeatability
gtmChallenges.challenge8: Initiatives are ad-hoc and short-term, with no continuous improvement cycle
gtmChallenges.challenge9: Market share is being lost to competitors
gtmChallenges.challenge10: Expansion into new markets or channels lacks preparation and stalls
gtmChallenges.challenge11: The business has hit a scaling wall, with no clear next growth driver
gtmChallenges.challenge12: Gap between leadership's strategy and frontline execution

C.heading: GTM Developing
C.description: Clear improvement areas, systematic approach needed

strategies.title: What is go to
strategies.studyTitle: According to a recent study by dervix(2025):
strategies.statisticText: of U.S. companies have already defined a Go-to-Market (GTM) strategy
strategies.source: Source: “25 Statistics to Influence Your 2025 GTM Plan” by Devrix

items.title: Define differentiation and messaging
items.problem1: Poor differentiation leads to price battles
items.problem2: No lasting impression of unique strengths

items.title: Clarify targeting and positioning
items.problem1: Lack of focus spreads resources thin
items.problem2: No clear path to win based on strengths

items.title: Define differentiation and messaging
items.problem1: Poor differentiation leads to price battles
items.problem2: No lasting impression of unique strengths

items.title: Design the offering & value proposition
items.problem2: Messaging fixates on features, not value

items.title: Execute, measure and optimize
items.problem1: Short-term chasing with no strategic tuning

1.text: Customer understanding is superficial, making it difficult to craft compelling value propositions or differentiate from competitors.

recommendation.smb: Start with direct customer interviews to identify pain points.
recommendation.mid-market: Improve data hygiene and segmentation in your CRM, and define ICP scoring criteria.
recommendation.enterprise: Build integrated market research capabilities across divisions.

2.text: Customer insights exist but are fragmented or insufficiently quantified, reducing the precision of strategic planning.

recommendation.smb: Use free survey tools to collect basic customer data.
recommendation.mid-market: Introduce structured data analysis and hire skilled analysts.
recommendation.enterprise: Leverage AI-driven platforms for advanced customer analytics.

1.text: Departments work in silos without shared KPIs, leading to inefficiency and misalignment.

recommendation.smb: Define simple, shared KPIs (e.g., leads per month) across sales and marketing.
recommendation.mid-market: Set up cross-functional project teams with unified goals.
recommendation.enterprise: Establish company-wide KPI dashboards and quarterly review meetings.

2.text: Some collaboration exists, but cross-functional alignment is weak and goals remain inconsistent.

recommendation.smb: Hold weekly joint meetings between sales and marketing.
recommendation.mid-market: Standardize reporting formats and unify departmental KPIs.

1.text: Pipeline management is ad-hoc, with unpredictable sales cycles and limited repeatability.

recommendation.smb: Track simple velocity metrics (lead-to-opportunity, close rate) manually.
recommendation.mid-market: Introduce CRM/SFA tools to monitor cycle times systematically.
recommendation.enterprise: Apply process mining and predictive analytics to optimize velocity.

2.text: Basic processes exist, but pipeline speed and conversion efficiency remain suboptimal.

recommendation.smb: Focus on reducing bottlenecks and simplifying workflows.
recommendation.mid-market: Standardize the sales process and expand use of automation tools.
recommendation.enterprise: Roll out AI-based forecasting and best-practice sharing across units.

1.text: Upsell/cross-sell efforts and new market initiatives are minimal, limiting revenue growth potential.

recommendation.smb: Begin with a simple upsell offer for existing customers.
recommendation.enterprise: Build a structured expansion motion across regions and business units. Strengthen partner ecosystems and strategic alliances.

2.text: Expansion strategies exist but are not systematic or consistently executed.

recommendation.smb: Document success cases and replicate simple upsell patterns.
recommendation.mid-market: Align cross-sell strategies across departments with shared targets.
recommendation.enterprise: Establish governance and operating cadence for expansion initiatives. Explore strategic alliances where gaps in coverage exist.

smb.D_C: Clarify roles between sales and marketing and set basic KPIs. Start simple KPI tracking using spreadsheets and review performance weekly to establish consistency.
smb.C_B: Launch weekly cross-functional syncs (Sales, Marketing, CS). Introduce shared customer data and set one or two common KPIs such as customer satisfaction.
smb.B_A: Introduce simple ROI analysis (CAC vs LTV). Use data from campaigns to refine strategies monthly, and gradually build predictive capabilities.
smb.A_S: Pursue strategic partnerships and explore new distribution channels (e.g., digital). Focus resources on niches where you can establish a dominant position.
smb.S: Codify your growth model and strengthen organizational scalability. Position your brand as an industry role model for innovation and agility.

mid-market.D_C: Formalize departmental KPIs with dashboards (Sales: win rate; Marketing: lead-to-SQL conversion). Consider implementing a CRM/MA tool for systematic tracking.
mid-market.C_B: Establish cross-department project teams. Align customer lifecycle KPIs and create forums for sharing best practices across departments.
mid-market.B_A: Hire or develop dedicated data analysts. Apply forecasting models and A/B testing to continuously optimize campaigns and pipeline performance.
mid-market.A_S: Explore adjacent markets and invest in new business areas. Build organizational capacity for digital transformation and innovation.
mid-market.S: Protect competitive advantage by institutionalizing unique know-how. Standardize innovations and position your company as an industry benchmark.

enterprise.D_C: Implement company-wide KPI systems with BI dashboards. Hold quarterly executive KPI reviews and ensure visibility across business units.
enterprise.C_B: Create cross-unit customer strategy committees. Unify customer experience design and institutionalize collaboration across business divisions.
enterprise.B_A: Build advanced business intelligence platforms. Leverage AI for customer behavior analysis and scenario-based strategic planning.
enterprise.A_S: Drive industry-level innovation by investing in disruptive technologies and new market creation. Position as a transformation leader.
enterprise.S: Shape global ecosystems and help define industry standards. Expand influence through strategic alliances and position your organization as a thought leader in your sector.
